header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Talent Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

items.text: Own and optimize {tools|e-learning platforms}—driving engagement, tracking usage, and building impactful {prog|learning paths}
Design and deliver engaging {prog|training sessions} for Business Professionals on performance, communication, and workplace skills
Partner with {cross|stakeholders} to implement training solutions, sourcing {coord|vendors or facilitators} and evaluating program effectiveness
Enhance and promote {prog|manager development tools}, equipping HRBPs and leaders with practical resources
Leverage {data|data and metrics} to evaluate learning programs, continuously improving the manager toolkit and leadership training
Support {prog|performance management} cycles, including facilitating CAP sessions and tracking key performance insights
Contribute to {prog|talent review} processes by maintaining talent data, profiles, and development planning resources
Play a key role in talent acquisition operations—managing intranet content, coordinating projects, maintaining {coord|vendor relationships}
Help deliver an exceptional {ex|candidate and new-hire experience} by supporting interview logistics, leading orientation programming
Bachelor's degree required; preferred fields: Business, Adult Education, or Human Resources
Minimum 3+ years of experience in HR functions, preferably {prog|Learning & Development}
Advanced proficiency in {tools|MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)}; familiarity with {tools|LMS}, HR systems, and {tools|ATS} strongly preferred
Strong facilitation and presentation skills, with experience leading both in-person and virtual training sessions
Strong {data|analytical skills} with ability to assess needs, prioritize, and align work with business objectives
